Rev: $25M: Understanding the legacy decision making process.

Interview questions: Inventory (Pain Points)
Client: Europena - Food Distribution: "You mentioned moving away from Fishbowl. What specifically made it frustrating or no longer worth using?"
Rev: $25M: Identifying the churn triggers for competitors.
Method Spend: ~USD7000/annual: Fishbowl data structure made everything come through as a Fishbowl item. Product descriptions were buried in a memo style field, making search, reporting, and downstream use in Method painful. They had to manually add descriptions and clean data, creating extra work.

Interview questions: Inventory (The Future)
Client: Europena - Food Distribution: "What specific feature or promise does Markov offer that made you confident enough to switch?"

Interview questions: Inventory (Ideal State)
